pilot_strategy: Three independent pilot streams executed in parallel: (1) P1 confound go/no-go is the critical path -- zero GPU, 15 min, determines whether H1 is worth pursuing. (2) P3 scaling surface data download + linear fit -- zero GPU, 20 min, validates SAEBench data availability. (3) P2 probe training pilot -- single GPU, 15 min, validates model access and probe quality.
go_nogo_logic: P1 go/no-go is the most important gate. If ALL four partial correlations drop below |0.2| after L0 control, pivot H1 narrative to 'L0 is the true driver.' P2 probe pilot determines whether cross-domain contribution (H2) is feasible. P3 pilot determines whether scaling surface (H3) has structure worth characterizing.

pilots.name: L0 Covariate Go/No-Go Pilot
pilots.description: Load iter_004 C3A_saebench_corr.json. Compute partial_corr(absorption, sparse_probing_f1 | log_width, layer, arch_class, log_L0) using pingouin. Compare with iter_004 result (partial_r = -0.661 without L0). This is the single most important pilot: it determines whether H1 is worth pursuing.
pilots.pass_criteria: partial_r for sparse_probing_f1 has |r| > 0.2 after adding log(L0) as covariate. If < 0.2 for ALL four metrics, H1 is falsified -- pivot to characterizing L0 as true driver.
pilots.failure_action: Pivot H1 to negative result framing: 'absorption is an epiphenomenon of L0.' Continue with H2 (cross-domain) and H3 (scaling surface) as primary contributions. Full Phase 1 still runs to quantify how much of the correlation is L0-driven.
pilots.key_check: Count SAEs with non-null L0 (expect ~48 of 54). If < 30, insufficient data for L0-controlled analysis.

pilots.name: Scaling Surface Data Availability Pilot
pilots.description: Download SAEBench results from HuggingFace. Count SAEs with both absorption score and L0 available. Fit simple linear model: absorption ~ log(width) + log(L0). Check R^2.
pilots.pass_criteria: N >= 100 SAEs with both absorption and L0. Linear model R^2 > 0.15. Data spans at least 3 width values and 4 L0 values.
pilots.failure_action: If SAEBench data unavailable or N < 100, fall back to the 54-SAE dataset from iter_004 for a reduced scaling analysis. Report limitation.
pilots.key_check: SAEBench HuggingFace dataset accessible and parseable. Absorption scores present in expected format.

pilots.name: RAVEL Probe Training Pilot on Gemma 2 2B
pilots.description: Load Gemma 2 2B via TransformerLens. Load 200 cities from RAVEL with country labels. Extract residual stream activations at layer 12. Train city-country logistic regression probe (1 seed). Measure accuracy.
pilots.pass_criteria: Gemma 2B loads without 401/access error. City-country probe at layer 12 achieves accuracy > 75% on held-out set of 200 cities. If model access fails, GPT-2 Small fallback probe achieves > 80%.
pilots.failure_action: If Gemma 2B access fails: fall back to GPT-2 Small with SAELens GPT-2 SAEs. If probe accuracy < 75% at layer 12: try layers 8 and 17. If all layers fail: try city-continent (simpler hierarchy). If all hierarchies fail: abort H2 cross-domain contribution.
pilots.key_check: HuggingFace token has Gemma 2 access. TransformerLens supports gemma-2-2b. RAVEL data downloadable.

pilots.name: Taxonomy Correction Feasibility Pilot
pilots.description: Load iter_004 C2D_taxonomy.json. Count letters with n_comparison_tokens=0. For 5 such letters, attempt frequency-matched comparison token sampling. Verify that frequency-matched tokens exist in sufficient quantity.
pilots.pass_criteria: Frequency-matched comparison tokens found for >= 4/5 test letters. Each has >= 10 comparison tokens.
pilots.failure_action: If frequency-matched sampling fails, use Chanin ground-truth parent feature IDs as alternative baseline. If both fail, report taxonomy correction as infeasible and retain original rates with caveats.
pilots.key_check: Taxonomy JSON has per-letter n_comparison_tokens field. Token frequency data accessible from model tokenizer.

execution_order.note: Zero-GPU pilots, run in parallel immediately
GPU pilot, can run in parallel with batch 1 if GPU available

P1_go.condition: At least 1 of 4 metrics retains |partial_r| > 0.2 after L0 control
P1_go.if_true: Proceed with full Phase 1 (width-stratified, mediation, Rosenbaum, etc.)
P1_go.if_false: Pivot H1 to negative result. Phase 1 becomes 'How much does L0 explain?' Reduce scope to go/no-go + mediation only.

P2_go.if_true: Proceed with full Phase 2 (absorption measurement, controls, cross-domain comparison)
P2_go.if_false: Abort Phase 2 if no model works. If only Gemma fails, use GPT-2 Small with reduced novelty.

P3_go.condition: N >= 100 SAEs with absorption + L0, linear R^2 > 0.15
P3_go.if_true: Proceed with full Phase 3 (GAM fitting, interaction test, phase boundary detection)
P3_go.if_false: Fall back to 54-SAE analysis. Reduced power but still informative.

paper_killed.condition: H1 falsified AND H2 infeasible (no model access + no probe quality)
paper_killed.if_true: Pivot to 'honest audit' framing (cand_honest_audit). Paper becomes deflationary: absorption is less important than believed.
paper_killed.if_false: Continue with at least 2 of 3 contributions.
